subsystem_boundary: Normal redis_commands::dispatch registration plus the HyperLogLog PFDEBUG debug surface. The packet keeps ACL, transactions, expiration, pub/sub, blocking wakeups, AOF, replication, RDB, and normal dispatcher semantics in place and does not rewrite PFADD, PFCOUNT, or PFMERGE.
implementation: Registered PFDEBUG in the shared dispatch table.
Implemented PFDEBUG GETREG, DECODE, ENCODING, TODENSE, and the SIMD status/toggle stub against the existing stored HLL bytes.
GETREG promotes sparse HLL values to dense before returning 16384 packed register values, matching Valkey's debug command behavior.
Dense register get/set now treats the byte after the packed dense slice as a zero sentinel instead of indexing past the Rust slice.
Updated TCL frontier docs as telemetry only; no public conformance claim was expanded.

Updated target/release/redis-server for smoke; completed with existing repository warnings.

non_gating_checks.notes: Workspace-wide check reports large pre-existing formatting diffs across unrelated crates; no formatting write was run.
Packet target files are not rustfmt-clean before this narrow packet; formatting them would create broad dispatch/HLL churn outside the behavioral fix.

residual_risk: PFSELFTEST remains outside this packet and still fails the focused TCL survey.
CONFIG SET hll-sparse-max-bytes sparse-to-dense behavior remains outside this packet and accounts for the other two focused TCL failures.
PFDEBUG SIMD remains a scalar-port status stub because no safe SIMD/global simd_enabled architecture exists yet.
This evidence is telemetry-only and does not change public conformance claims.
